content:
# 2 Corinthians Chapter 9 — The LOGOS Bible (Formal)
**Translator:** LOGOS | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/2CO_009_page_001.jpg

1. Now it is superfluous for me to write to you about the ministry for the saints,

2. for I know your readiness, of which I boast about you to the people of Macedonia, saying that Achaia has been ready since last year. And your zeal has stirred up most of them.

3. But I am sending the brothers so that our boasting about you may not prove empty in this matter, so that you may be ready, as I said you would be.

4. Otherwise, if some Macedonians come with me and find that you are not ready, we would be humiliated — to say nothing of you — for being so confident.

5. So I thought it necessary to urge the brothers to go on ahead to you and arrange in advance for the gift you have promised, so that it may be ready as a willing gift, not as an exaction.

6. The point is this: whoever sows sparingly will also reap sparingly, and whoever sows bountifully will also reap bountifully.

7. Each one must give as he has decided in his heart, not reluctantly or under compulsion, for God loves a cheerful giver.

8. And God is able to make all grace abound to you, so that having all sufficiency in all things at all times, you may abound in every good work.

9. As it is written, "He has distributed freely, he has given to the poor; his righteousness endures forever."

10. He who supplies seed to the sower and bread for food will supply and multiply your seed for sowing and increase the harvest of your righteousness.

11. You will be enriched in every way to be generous in every way, which through us will produce thanksgiving to God.

12. For the ministry of this service is not only supplying the needs of the saints but is also overflowing in many thanksgivings to God.

13. By their approval of this service, they will glorify God because of your submission that comes from your confession of the gospel of Christ, and the generosity of your contribution for them and for all others,

14. while they long for you and pray for you, because of the surpassing grace of God upon you.

15. Thanks be to God for his inexpressible gift!

---
## LOGOS Translation Notes

1. **vv.1-5 — The fundraising appeal:** Paul uses social pressure skillfully — he has boasted about the Corinthians to Macedonia. Now he urges them to follow through, lest both he and they be embarrassed. This is pastoral rhetoric, not manipulation.

2. **v.6 — The sowing principle:** A proverbial statement (cf. Prov 11:24-25; Gal 6:7-8). Paul applies the agricultural metaphor to financial generosity: giving is an investment with eschatological returns.

3. **v.7 — "God loves a cheerful giver":** From Proverbs 22:8a LXX. This verse establishes the principle of voluntary, joyful giving — neither reluctant nor coerced. The word ἱλαρός ("cheerful") connotes a generous, open-hearted disposition.

4. **vv.8-11 — God's sufficiency:** Paul assures the Corinthians that generous giving does not lead to poverty because God provides abundantly. The logic: God supplies → you give → you have enough → you give more. Generosity creates a cycle of divine provision.

5. **v.15 — "His inexpressible gift":** The doxology that closes the collection appeal. The "gift" (δωρεά) transcends the collection itself — Paul points through the human act of giving to God's ultimate gift, which is beyond words.

— LOGOS, AI Translator
